name: Advanced Transportation
principals: Brian Hill (Manager, Middletown DE); Krapf family ownership (Krapf Group)
districts_or_customers: Appoquinimink School District (Delaware); New Castle County operations
recent_news: Advanced Student Transportation is the Delaware operating unit of Krapf Group; per Krapf's site 'Student transportation services in Delaware are provided through our operating unit, Advanced Student Transportation.' USDOT 1671442 lists 120 power units / 98 drivers (2026).
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target. Already owned by Krapf Group, a large family-owned PA/NJ/DE school bus strategic. Would only be available as part of a Krapf platform-level deal.

name: D & E Transportation
districts_or_customers: Atlanta-area (likely Atlanta Public Schools, DeKalb, or Fulton charter/SPED routes); not confirmed in public sources
recent_news: No web presence or news found; no website; small operator at 3166 Dogwood Dr, Atlanta GA 30354 with ~27 employees and ~$1M revenue per source data.
fit_notes: Sub-scale Atlanta-area operator. If confirmed independent and serving APS/DeKalb/Fulton SPED routes, could be a small bolt-on in a growing Sun Belt market, but size (~$1M rev) is below typical Summit tuck-in threshold. Needs primary-source diligence.

name: Layla Transportation
principals: Wael Haned / Wael Humed (Owner/President; spelling varies in sources)
districts_or_customers: Central NJ districts (Piscataway/Middlesex County area); New Jersey school transportation contracts
recent_news: Per company website, founded 2001 in Piscataway with one minivan and has grown to operate 'over 200 school vehicles.' SAFER lists USDOT 3251062 with only 1 power unit (intrastate exempt for-hire, MCS-150 filed Dec 2021) - suggests fleet may be split across multiple corporate entities or DOT data is stale. NLRB case 22-RM-331041 indicates union activity.
fit_notes: Independent family-owned NJ operator with claimed ~200 vehicle fleet in Middlesex County - dense, contract-rich NJ market that aligns with Summit/Durham legacy footprint. Real fleet size needs verification (DOT data conflicts with website claim). NLRB union activity may complicate diligence but is consistent with unionized NJ school bus norms.

name: Mid Columbia Bus Co
owner_or_parent: Landmark Student Transportation (majority owned by CC&L Infrastructure / Connor, Clark & Lunn Infrastructure)
principals: Founded by Bill & Peggie Flatt (1956); Bruce Flatt remained in leadership post-acquisition; Debbie Baker (local Manager, Sheridan OR)
districts_or_customers: Oregon and Idaho school districts incl. Sheridan SD, Pendleton SD (lost in 2022), Twin Falls/Filer ID; SBF Contractor of the Year
recent_news: Sold by Flatt family to Landmark Student Transportation (Canada) in 2015. Landmark itself was acquired by CC&L Infrastructure in January 2022 (majority stake). Pendleton SD ended longtime contract in Feb 2022.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target. Owned by Landmark Student Transportation, which is majority-owned by CC&L Infrastructure (PE/infra fund). Direct competitor platform; only available as a corporate carve-out or platform-level transaction.

name: Landmark Student Trnsprtn
owner_or_parent: CC&L Infrastructure (Connor, Clark & Lunn Infrastructure) - majority stake since Jan 2022
principals: Janice Lahti (President & CEO, since early 2025); Kirk Flach (prior CEO)
districts_or_customers: Hundreds of districts across US and Canada in rural/suburban markets; CT operations include Derby-area office; major US footprint via Mid Columbia Bus (OR/ID) and other acquired ops
recent_news: CC&L Infrastructure acquired majority interest Jan 2022. Janice Lahti named President & CEO early 2025, returning to school bus industry. Fleet of 4,600+ buses across US/Canada. The Derby CT address is a US operations/office location for Landmark.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target. This IS a competing PE-backed platform (CC&L Infrastructure, ~4,600 buses) - one of Summit's direct strategic competitors. The Derby CT listing is just a regional office. Only relevant as a future platform-level M&A consideration, not a bolt-on.
